$comment: CVE -> Intune Win32 app mapping for KEV third-party remediation. The Logic App reads this to look up which packaged Intune app fixes a given CVE.

mappings.notes: Replace intuneAppId with the GUID from your Intune tenant after uploading the .intunewin (Apps -> Windows -> Add -> Windows app (Win32)). cveId is a placeholder until a Windows endpoint with 7-Zip is onboarded to MDE - replace with the actual KEV-listed 7-Zip CVE when MDETVM_CL surfaces a real row.
Sample - replace with real GUID after packaging.
Sample - use Google Chrome Enterprise MSI, not the consumer installer.

schema.cveId: string  - CISA KEV CVE ID, exact match against MDETVM_CL.cveId
schema.softwareVendor: string  - lowercase vendor token, must match MDETVM_CL.softwareVendor
schema.softwareName: string  - lowercase software token, must match MDETVM_CL.softwareName
schema.intuneAppId: GUID    - mobileApps id of the .intunewin already uploaded to Intune
schema.intuneAppDisplayName: string  - human-readable name for notifications and audit logs
schema.minRemediatedVersion: string  - the version that resolves the CVE; used for KQL proof
schema.supersedesAppIds: GUID[] - optional, list of older mobileApps ids this app supersedes (informational only when Intune supersedence is configured on the app itself)

lookupContract.secondaryKey: softwareVendor + softwareName  - used when MDE returns a different CVE flavor or a vendor-grouped recommendation
lookupContract.missMode: When no mapping exists, the Logic App must fall through to the existing notification path (email + Teams) and NOT attempt an assign.
